heading: Parental consent requirement for nonemergency intrusive physical examinations
label: 9852a Parental consent requirement for nonemergency intrusive physical examinations

children.text: is not immediately necessary to protect the health or safety of the child involved or the health or safety of another individual; and
requires incision or is otherwise invasive, or involves exposure of private body parts.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: b Requirement
children.text: A Head Start agency shall obtain written parental consent before administration of any nonemergency intrusive physical examination of a child in connection with participation in a program under this subchapter.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: c Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit agencies from using established methods, for handling cases of suspected or known child abuse and neglect, that are in compliance with applicable Federal, State, or tribal law.
